metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the Files entry 'eval spec + fixtures …' with concrete file paths (e.g. `tests/eval/api-compat/spec.yaml`, `tests/eval/api-compat/fixtures/`) and specify the expected file format.
findings.finding: The second Files entry is a description, not a file path, leaving the executor to invent the eval-spec and fixture file locations without guidance.
findings.location: Files — 'eval spec + fixtures for the api-compat overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add explicit paths such as `tests/eval/api-compat/api-compat-spec.yaml` and `tests/eval/api-compat/fixtures/` (mirroring any existing eval structure in the repo), and state whether fixtures are YAML, JSON, or Markdown.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Quantify AC2: specify the proving command (e.g. `rebar eval run api-compat`), a minimum recall count (e.g. all N recall fixtures must fire), and a maximum FP suppression count (e.g. 0 no-fire cases suppressed).
findings.finding: AC2's 'passing recall + no-fire eval' provides no numeric threshold or proving command, making it impossible to verify objectively.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC2 as: 'Running `<eval-command>` reports: all recall fixtures fire (N/N); 0 no-fire fixtures suppressed. Evidence: eval output attached to PR.' Adjust N to the actual fixture count once files are specified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `eval_solver.run_case` supports `code-review-api-compat` as a prompt id (it currently raises `ValueError`), and if not, add the missing solver arm or execution path to scope so AC2 ('passing recall + no-fire eval') is achievable.
findings.finding: The plan names 'eval spec + fixtures for the api-compat overlay' as a deliverable and AC2 asserts it will yield 'a passing recall + no-fire eval', but no `code-review-api-compat.eval.yaml` (or any `code-review-*.eval.yaml`) exists in `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`, AND `eval_solver.run_case` has no execution arm for `code-review-api-compat` as a prompt id — it raises `ValueError` for any unknown non-criterion id. The plan implicitly assumes eval infrastructure for code-review overlays already exists or needs only fixtures, but both the spec file and the solver arm are absent.
findings.location: Files section ('eval spec + fixtures for the api-compat overlay') / AC2 ('Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval')
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a `code-review-api-compat` arm to `eval_solver.run_case` that routes through the live code-review gate runner, or (b) reframe the eval as an offline fixture test (e.g., a pytest parametrized case) and update AC2 to name that mechanism explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the unresolvable 'dso content' reference with a concrete, locatable source artifact (e.g., a specific file path, ADR, or inline enumeration of the recall patterns and FP-guard conditions the overlay should carry).
findings.finding: The plan's What section states the enrichment will be drawn from 'dso content' ('Enrich the api-compat overlay prompt with dso content: recall patterns AND FP-guards'), treating 'dso content' as a named, locatable source artifact. Searched `src/rebar/llm/` for 'dso' — zero matches. The only 'dso' hits in the entire repo are in experiment harnesses (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/`) and an unrelated reconciler test fixture, none of which constitute a retrievable content source for api-compat recall patterns or FP-guards.
findings.location: What section: 'Enrich the api-compat overlay prompt with dso content: recall patterns AND FP-guards (both halves)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the concrete file path for the eval spec to the Files section (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-api-compat.eval.yaml` or `.rebar/evals/code-review-api-compat.eval.yaml`) so the executor places it where `load_eval_spec` can discover it.
findings.finding: The second Files entry ('eval spec + fixtures for the api-compat overlay') has no concrete file path, and no `code-review-api-compat.eval.yaml` (nor any `code-review-*.eval.yaml`) exists anywhere in the repo (`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` and `.rebar/evals/` contain only plan-review-* and unrelated specs). This is a hallucinated/missing edit target.
findings.location: Files section, line 2: 'eval spec + fixtures for the api-compat overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-api-compat.eval.yaml` (or `.rebar/evals/code-review-api-compat.eval.yaml` if a repo-local override is intended) as an explicit entry in the Files section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `eval_solver.run_case()` (in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py`) as a required edit target — or explicitly note an alternative harness mechanism — so the executor knows how to make the code-review-api-compat eval spec runnable.
findings.finding: `eval_solver.run_case()` has no execution arm for `code-review-api-compat`. The `_criterion_id` resolver only matches plan-review criteria; a `code-review-api-compat` prompt_id falls through to `raise ValueError(f'no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}')`. AC2's 'passing recall + no-fire eval' therefore requires either a new solver arm or a different harness mechanism — an unenumerated dependency the plan does not acknowledge.
findings.location: AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'; Files section (eval spec deliverable)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` to the Files section (read-only / no-edit-needed annotation is fine) to acknowledge it as an outside-dir consumer of the overlay.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` (lines 79, 107) is an outside-dir consumer that references `code-review-api-compat` by prompt id. While the plan's enrichment (adding recall patterns + FP-guards to the overlay prompt text) is backward-compatible with this consumer, it is not enumerated in Files or the plan body.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` to Files with a note: `# consumer — no edit required for prompt-text enrichment`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for WHY inline prompt-text enrichment is the right structural mechanism (e.g., consistent with all peer overlays — security, db-migrations, performance — which inline domain patterns rather than externalising to a separate rubric file).
findings.finding: The plan's positive rationale ('locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay') explains WHERE enrichment belongs but does not state WHY inline prompt-text enrichment is the right structural mechanism — e.g., alignment with every other overlay in the codebase (security, performance, db-migrations, llm-prompts all inline domain knowledge rather than externalizing it), which is the actual justification for the approach.
findings.location: Plan section: What / 'per the locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, append: 'Patterns and guards are inlined directly into the overlay prompt text (consistent with all other specialist overlays in src/rebar/llm/reviewers/, which carry domain knowledge inline rather than via an external rubric file).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `run_case` execution arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs to `eval_solver.py` (or document an alternative eval mechanism), and name it explicitly in the plan — without this, AC2's 'passing recall + no-fire eval' cannot execute.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' for the api-compat overlay, but `eval_solver.run_case()` has no execution arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs: it handles only `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment` explicitly, routes plan-review criterion IDs via `_criterion_id`, and raises `ValueError` for everything else. `code-review-api-compat` is neither a plan-review criterion nor one of the three agentic reviewers, so a live recall/no-fire eval cannot execute with the current harness.
findings.location: AC2 — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) extend `eval_solver.run_case` with a `code-review-api-compat` arm that assembles a synthetic diff and calls the overlay runner, mirroring the agentic-reviewer pattern; or (b) scope AC2 to offline spec-structure validation only (`rebar prompt eval code-review-api-compat` passes `validate_eval_spec`) and defer the live recall gate to a follow-on story that adds the harness arm.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the `code-review-api-compat` prompt will be wired into the eval harness — either add a solver arm in `eval_solver.run_case()` for code-review overlays or call out an alternate eval mechanism (e.g., a standalone fixture runner), since the current dispatcher raises `ValueError` for unrecognized prompt ids.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 ('passing recall + no-fire eval') depends on a working eval harness for the code-review-api-compat prompt, but eval_solver.run_case() has no dispatch arm for code-review overlay prompts — only 'completion-verifier', 'ticket-quality', 'spec-alignment', and plan-review criterion IDs are handled; any other prompt_id raises ValueError. The plan does not specify whether a new solver arm is needed or a different eval mechanism applies.
findings.location: AC: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py lines 136-171

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the FP-guard vocabulary for the api-compat overlay — enumerate at least the named FP modes (e.g., internal-rename-not-public-surface, additive-only-no-removal, versioned-break-with-explicit-migration) that the overlay must NOT fire on, mirroring how the plan-review finder eval names its FP taxonomy.
findings.finding: The FP-guard vocabulary for the api-compat domain is referenced as a required deliverable ('FP-guards (both halves)') but is never defined anywhere in the plan. Unlike the plan-review finder eval, which defines named FP modes (domain-inappropriate-standard-import, library-guaranteed-capability, etc.), the api-compat plan names no specific FP patterns — leaving the executor to invent them without grounding.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section: 'FP-guards (both halves)'; AC1: 'api-compat overlay carries both recall patterns and FP-discrimination guards'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the concrete eval spec file path to the Files section (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-api-compat.eval.yaml`) and confirm which prompt id string governs `load_eval_spec` resolution for code-review overlays.
findings.finding: The plan's Files section lists 'eval spec + fixtures for the api-compat overlay' as a deliverable but provides no file path. The eval infrastructure resolves specs via a naming convention (eval_specs/<prompt-id>.eval.yaml or .rebar/evals/<prompt-id>.eval.yaml), but the prompt-id for this overlay is 'rebar-code-review-api-compat' (the langfuse_prompt value) or 'code-review-api-compat' (the dimension value) — the plan does not resolve which id governs the eval lookup, leaving the executor to guess.
findings.location: Plan 'Files' section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the api-compat overlay'; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-api-compat.md lines 10-11
findings.suggested_fix: src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-api-compat.eval.yaml (matching the `dimension:` value, consistent with how `code-quality.eval.yaml` is named for the `code-quality` prompt id).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the overlay prompt what the model should do when the consumer-grep sub-step returns no results or errors (e.g., 'if no consumers are found, note the absence as evidence but do not fire a finding solely on that basis'), co-locating the fallback rule with the grep instruction.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the overlay to 'grep consumers of changed public surface' as a recall pattern, but defines no fallback for when this agentic sub-step is incomplete (e.g., grep finds nothing, the repository is too large, or the file tool errors). No decision rule is co-located in the overlay or plan for how to handle absent consumer evidence.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section: 'grep consumers of changed public surface'; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-api-compat.md (current file has no such instruction)

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
